frameworks.name: deep linear networks
frameworks.one_line: Tractable linear connectionist model used to derive exact early-learning dynamics.

frameworks.name: optimal constant solution (OCS)
frameworks.one_line: Input-independent label-marginal response identified as an early-learning attractor.

frameworks.name: hierarchical category learning
frameworks.one_line: Task framework used to probe both networks and human learners.

core_question: Do deep networks and humans pass through an early phase of producing the optimal constant (input-independent label-marginal) solution before learning the target function?
key_assumptions: Bias terms are present even when initialized to zero
Target label distribution is non-uniform and informative
Linear-network dynamics generalize qualitatively to nonlinear CNNs and humans
manipulations: Architecture: deep linear vs nonlinear CNN
Dataset: hierarchical MNIST and CIFAR10 category structures
Learner type: artificial network vs human across three days
key_findings: Early in training, network outputs mirror the target label distribution while ignoring inputs
Deep linear networks with bias provably converge first to the OCS
OCS hallmarks appear in nonlinear CNNs on hierarchical MNIST/CIFAR10
Human learners show the same early OCS signature in true-negative rate dynamics
interpretations: Early OCS reliance is a generic feature of gradient-based hierarchical learning
Shared learning signature suggests a common dynamical motif across networks and humans
